description: Edge cases and boundary condition scenarios

scenarios.name: Card Portfolio Upgrade with Cancellation
scenarios.description: User with suboptimal cards who should cancel and upgrade. Recalibrated 2026-07-01 against the allocation-consistent engine + S3 (realistic fixture bonus requirements, bonus capacity, zero-fee keep policy).

test_conditions: Basic Travel Card kept: allocation still nets +$1 (96 rewards vs 95 fee)
Better alternatives recommended on top rather than forcing a cancel
Zero-fee Low Reward Card is never a cancel candidate

scenarios.name: Portfolio Expansion - Add Complementary Cards
scenarios.description: User with good cards who should add complementary cards for better coverage

test_conditions: Keeps existing good cards
Adds cards for uncovered high-spend categories
Maximizes rewards across all spending categories

scenarios.name: Amazon-Only Spending - Single Card Optimization
scenarios.description: User spends only on Amazon, should recommend only Amazon Prime card. Recalibrated 2026-07-01 against the allocation-consistent engine + S3 (realistic fixture bonus requirements, bonus capacity, zero-fee keep policy).

card_count_optimization.reason: Amazon Prime wins the category; two bonus cards still net positive after shift costs

test_conditions: Only recommends cards that add positive value
Rejects multi-card portfolios with negative net value
Optimizes for single category spending pattern
Amazon Prime card provides best rate for Amazon spending

scenarios.name: Very Low Spending - Zero Cards Expected
scenarios.description: Minimal spending that doesn't justify any annual fees. Recalibrated 2026-07-01 against the allocation-consistent engine + S3 (realistic fixture bonus requirements, bonus capacity, zero-fee keep policy).

card_count_optimization.reason: One $0-fee card earning $18/yr; every fee card and every bonus is honestly out of reach

test_conditions: Returns zero recommendations when no profitable portfolio exists
Prevents recommending money-losing card combinations
Low spending doesn't justify annual fees
Empty portfolio preferred over negative value portfolio

scenarios.name: Only No-Fee Cards Justified
scenarios.description: Medium spending that only justifies no annual fee cards. Recalibrated 2026-07-01 against the allocation-consistent engine + S3 (realistic fixture bonus requirements, bonus capacity, zero-fee keep policy).

card_count_optimization.reason: Best no-fee card plus a grocery card whose bonus and 6x make its $95 fee positive even ongoing (+$85/yr)

test_conditions: Selects best no-fee option over fee cards
Medium spending doesn't justify premium annual fees
Portfolio value remains positive with no-fee cards
Rejects fee cards that would create negative value

scenarios.name: High Fee Portfolio Prevention
scenarios.description: Edge case where multiple fee cards would create negative portfolio. Recalibrated 2026-07-01 against the allocation-consistent engine + S3 (realistic fixture bonus requirements, bonus capacity, zero-fee keep policy).

card_count_optimization.reason: Only the $0-fee card is positive: both premium bonuses are unreachable at this spending

test_conditions: Prevents multiple premium cards with high combined fees
Selects single card that provides best value
Rejects premium card combinations that exceed spending benefits
Portfolio optimization prevents fee-heavy recommendations

scenarios.name: Category Optimization Validation
scenarios.description: Test that category rewards are properly allocated to best card only

test_conditions: Each category allocated to exactly one best card
No double counting of category spending across cards
Portfolio value reflects optimized allocation
